keyPoints: Core question framing: AI mediation heightening parasocial attachments and crowding out offline engagement.
Experimental design clarity: longitudinal RCT with high vs low AI mediation across 6–12 months.
Outcome measurement map: parasocial attachment (MMPR/PSI/PSR), offline social activity, loneliness (UCLA, MSPSS, F-SozU), cross-cultural validity.
Mediators and moderators: perceived AI empathy, relatedness needs, need to belong, culture, baseline loneliness.
Safety and ethics guardrails: AI chaperone overlays, privacy protections, preregistration, protocol transparency.
Measurement instruments plan: MMPR Polish adaptation, SCI PED/PSD, validated loneliness scales, objective proxies for offline activity.
Practical implications: design countermeasures to sustain real-world social engagement if crowding out emerges; craft responsible AI guidance.
